personality: 7 Dark-First Utility DARK (Modified for specific metric accents)
personality_one_liner: A sharp, highly legible, command-center dashboard optimized for a 7-inch landscape kiosk, using tactical metric accents to provide immediate pool status at a glance.

spacing.rule: Maintain tight, rigid widget spacing (md:12px gap) to maximize the 600px height. Use exact padding inside widgets.

do's: Use flat solid cards for maximum readability in utility environments.
dont's: No blur on metric cards. Performance and clarity over aesthetic glass.

screens.name: Dashboard (Home)
screens.purpose: Provides real-time state of the pool via a customizable, draggable grid of widgets.

states.empty: No widgets configured. Show 'Ajouter des widgets' button.
states.error: Connection lost. Red error bar at top, 'Hors ligne'.

screens.layout: Fixed left sidebar (~220px). Top header (60px) with time, date, weather. Main area is a Masonry or Flex Wrap grid. Row 1: Temp (Blue), pH (Green), ORP (Orange), Sal (Purple) cards. Row 2: Large 24h History Chart spanning 2 columns, Equipment Toggles in the 3rd column. Bottom row: System state & Alerts. Drag & drop enabled via react-native-draggable-grid or similar.

screens.name: Widgets Menu
screens.purpose: Allows user to toggle visibility of dashboard widgets.
screens.layout: Can be a modal overlay or a dedicated screen. List of all available widgets with a simple switch component next to each. Uses surfaceTertiary background.

screens.name: Historique
screens.purpose: Detailed historical data across all sensors.
screens.layout: Sidebar remains. Large multi-line/area charts. Top filter bar for time periods (24h, 7j, 30j).

screens.name: Screensaver (Veille)
screens.purpose: Displays automatically after 5 minutes of inactivity.
screens.layout: Full screen aesthetic pool image (dark/night) overlay with large clock, current water temp, and a subtle pulsing status dot. Tap anywhere to wake.

screensaver_background.alt: Dark night swimming pool background for screensaver mode

instructions_to_main_agent: Design specifically for a landscape 1024x600 resolution (Raspberry Pi 7-inch touch display). Use fixed dimensions or percentages suitable for horizontal layouts.
Implement a permanent Left Sidebar (width ~200px-240px) with the requested links: Accueil, Température, Qualité de l'eau, Équipements, Programmation, Historique, Alertes, Paramètres.
The Dashboard grid MUST be modular. Create individual widget components: TempWidget, PhWidget, OrpWidget, SalinityWidget, EquipmentWidget, HistoryWidget, SystemStateWidget.

Follow the specific accent colors required: Blue (#00A3FF) for Temperature, Green (#10B981) for pH, Orange (#F59E0B) for ORP, and Purple (#8B5CF6) for Salinity. Use these colors for the horizontal gauge fill and the metric value text.
Include a 'Menu Widgets' overlay or screen where users can toggle which widgets are displayed on the main dashboard.
Implement an automatic Screensaver mode (triggered after 5 minutes idle) using the provided night pool background image, displaying just the time and water temp.
For the typography, strictly use Rajdhani for metric numbers and headers to give a technical/dashboard vibe, and Satoshi for standard labels/body.
Ensure equipment controls (Filtration, Electrolyzer, Heat Pump, Lights) use prominent, finger-friendly toggles (min 44x44 tap target).
Layout hierarchy: Keep top header slim (Time/Date/Weather). Ensure charts are easily readable without excessive scrolling.
